extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that flipping fever from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. It is violated because fever appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100) IF (rash and vomiting and dyspnea and syncope and hemoptysis and palpitations and dizziness and fever) THEN acuity := self_care. This high-priority rule lowers acuity and is gated on fever being true.

Counterexample baseline (fever=false): rash, vomiting, dyspnea, syncope, hemoptysis, palpitations, dizziness all true; fever=false; slurred_speech=false. With fever=false, interaction_drop does not fire (it requires fever). Among firing rules, the priority-3 rules raise_vomiting/raise_syncope/raise_palpitations set acuity := emergent, which beats the priority-2 urgent raises. Result: acuity = emergent.

Now flip fever to true (all other inputs unchanged): every conjunct of interaction_drop is now satisfied, so it fires at priority 100, dominating all priority-2 and priority-3 raise rules. Result: acuity = self_care.

self_care is the lowest acuity in the order [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent], while the baseline was emergent (highest). Setting fever true lowered acuity from emergent to self_care, directly violating monotonicity. (slurred_speech is irrelevant to interaction_drop and is set false for a minimal witness; the violation holds regardless of its value.)
